steps.title: Connect warmth to wing size
steps.content: The assertion needs data showing that a warmer month is associated with a larger wing centroid size, because wing size is used as a proxy for body size and body size correlates with life span.

steps.title: Rule out irrelevant comparisons
steps.content: The fitting data compare May with July males, where the warmer month has a larger wing centroid size. B compares females with males rather than warmer and cooler months. C gives only a temperature difference without wing-size data. D does not support warmer months having larger size. The answer is A.
